channel.title: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism.
channel.description: KillBait is a news aggregator developed by students and professionals in journalism and web development from Argentina, Spain, and the United Kingdom. Our focus is on providing readers with clear, concise, and relevant summaries of each news story.

item.title: What is KillBait?
item.description: Surely you've clicked on a news article with an eye-catching or sensational headline...

item.title: How KillBait works
item.description: Our editors, along with any registered user, can submit news they find interesting simply by providing the URL to the system.

item.description: Common Questions about KillBait

item.title: Upcoming news
Marriott Homes & Villas Offers 10% Discount on 2+ Night Stays with Stackable Promotions
item.description: Marriott's Homes & Villas platform is offering a promotion that gives travelers a 10% discount when booking stays of two or more consecutive nights. This offer is valid for bookings made from February 16, 2026, to April 5, 2026, for stays completed between April 1, 2026, and July 31, 2026. The promotion applies to cash or credit card bookings, Marriott Bonvoy points redemptions, and mixed bookings, though Free Night Awards are excluded. Notably, registration is not required, and the discount is automatically applied at checkout for participating homes. This promotion can also be stacked with up to three other ongoing offers: earning 25,000 bonus points for a first Homes & Villas booking (with a two-night minimum and $1,500 spend), earning 2,000 bonus points per night for stays above $2,000 (up to 10,000 points), and a new Marriott promotion offering 2,500 bonus points per stay plus one elite night per brand. Additionally, rewards can be maximized by using shopping portals or browser ex

item.title: Comprehensive Review of the American Express Blue Business Plus Credit Card
item.description: The American Express Blue Business Plus Credit Card is a versatile business card ideal for small business owners or anyone who values Membership Rewards points. It offers 2x points on all eligible purchases up to $50,000 per year, with 1x points thereafter, and has no annual fee. Points earned can be transferred to a variety of airline and hotel partners, making it highly flexible for travel redemptions. While the card’s points are worth approximately 1.5 cents each when redeemed for travel, cash back redemption is less favorable unless combined with certain American Express Platinum products. Additionally, the card provides purchase protections for up to $1,000 per claim and pools points across all Membership Rewards accounts under the primary cardholder. However, there is a 2.7% foreign transaction fee, and cash back redemptions outside certain accounts are less valuable. Overall, the Blue Business Plus is recommended as a long-term business credit card for those seeking a simple, fe

item.title: JetBlue Offers Double Mosaic Tiles on $100+ One-Way Flights for Limited Time
item.description: JetBlue is offering a limited-time promotion allowing travelers to earn double Tiles toward Mosaic elite status for qualifying one-way flights costing $100 or more. This promotion is valid for bookings made on February 17 and 18, 2026, and applies to travel from February 24 through March 18, 2026. Only flights operated by JetBlue and purchased directly via jetblue.com or the JetBlue app qualify; bookings through third-party sites or travel agencies are excluded. Each one-way segment must meet a minimum fare of $100 before taxes and fees to earn one Tile, and bonus Tiles are awarded in an amount equal to the base Tiles earned. Round trips are eligible but are calculated per one-way segment, and connecting flights do not qualify. Blue Basic fares, TrueBlue points redemptions, and JetBlue Vacations bookings are also excluded. Bonus Tiles are awarded 4–6 weeks after travel completion and are separate from TrueBlue points. There is no cap on the number of qualifying segments eligible for do

item.title: Capital One Cardholders Can Earn 16x Miles or 16% Cashback on In-Store Belk Purchases
item.description: Capital One currently offers a special promotion for targeted cardholders to earn 16x miles or 16% cashback on in-store purchases at Belk. Each linked Capital One card can activate the offer up to three times, and the deal is valid for one week after activation. While the official terms exclude gift card purchases, Belk stores often carry third-party gift cards such as Amazon, Best Buy, and Visa/Mastercard options, which may still qualify for the 16x earning in practice. This makes the promotion particularly appealing for those looking to maximize rewards through gift card purchases. To participate, cardholders must log in to their Capital One account, locate the Belk offer under 'Activate shopping offers,' and ensure activation before the February 23 expiration date. The offer may also be available on multiple cards, allowing for three activations per card. While there is no stated cap on earnings, users should be aware that the payout on gift cards is not guaranteed. The promotion hi

item.title: Jada Pinkett Smith Files Motion to Dismiss Bilaal Salaam’s $3 Million Lawsuit Alleging Threats
item.description:
Jada Pinkett Smith has moved to dismiss a $3 million lawsuit filed against her by musician Bilaal Salaam, rejecting his allegations as false and unsubstantiated. According to court documents cited in the report, Pinkett Smith argues that Salaam’s claims are part of an effort to generate attention and amount to harassment directed at her and her family. She further contends that the lawsuit attempts to penalize her for exercising constitutionally protected speech on matters involving her family.
Salaam filed the lawsuit in November 2025, alleging that Pinkett Smith confronted and threatened him during Will Smith’s birthday celebration on September 25, 2021, at the Regency Calabasas Common in California. Salaam, who describes himself as Will Smith’s close friend of nearly four decades, claims Pinkett Smith warned him he would “end up missing or catch a bullet” if he continued discussing her personal matters. Pinkett Smith denies making any such threats and states in her motion that she

item.title: Stephen Colbert Jokes About Affair Rumors Involving Corey Lewandowski and Kristi Noem
item.description:
Late-night host Stephen Colbert devoted part of his Monday monologue to renewed rumors alleging a romantic relationship between Corey Lewandowski and Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem. Speculation about the two political figures has circulated for months, dating back to early 2025 when Lewandowski was reportedly seen at Noem’s residence. The rumors have since become a frequent topic of discussion in Washington political circles.
Colbert referenced a recent Wall Street Journal report claiming that Noem and Lewandowski traveled together on a Boeing 737 MAX aircraft equipped with a private cabin and reportedly used by the Department of Homeland Security. The report also alleged that the pair flew together on other DHS-funded planes. One anecdote described an incident in which Lewandowski allegedly fired a pilot after a blanket belonging to Noem was left behind during a maintenance-related plane change, though the pilot was later rehired. Additionally, the New York Post reported tha

item.title: Swiss Olympic commentator criticises Israeli athlete’s past Gaza war comments during Winter Games broadcast
item.description:
Coverage of the Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics sparked controversy after a commentator on Swiss public broadcaster RTS criticised an Israeli athlete’s past social media statements about the war in Gaza. During the men’s bobsleigh event, commentator Stefan Renna referred to comments made by Israeli team captain Adam Edelman, who has previously described Israel’s military campaign in Gaza as the “most morally just war in history” and expressed support for members of the Israeli Defence Force deployed in the territory.
While Edelman was completing his second run, Renna cited findings from a United Nations inquiry published in September, which concluded that Israel had met four of the five criteria required to constitute genocide under international law. Renna also questioned Edelman’s eligibility to compete, referencing International Olympic Committee (IOC) rules applied to Russian athletes that barred those who actively supported the war in Ukraine. He contrasted this with a separa

item.title: Haier’s Ownership Model and the Diversity of Corporate Structures in China
item.description:
The article examines the global scale and ownership structure of Haier, the Chinese home appliance giant, using it as a starting point to reflect on broader corporate models in China. It challenges the common European perception of Haier as a secondary or emerging brand, emphasizing that Haier is in fact the world’s largest manufacturer of white goods. The company has expanded through acquisitions such as Italy’s Candy and General Electric’s appliance division, and now surpasses major European competitors like Electrolux and Bosch in sales volume. The piece also highlights the industrial concentration in regions such as Shunde (Foshan), where key microwave components are produced by firms like Welling (owned by Midea) and Galanz, illustrating China’s dominance in global supply chains.
Haier Smart Home is publicly listed in Shanghai, Hong Kong, and Frankfurt, with international institutional investors among its shareholders. However, its controlling stake is held by Haier Group, which,

item.title: TRUMP’S CONCENTRATION CAMPS BUILD INCINERATORS | The Kyle Kulinski Show
item.description: The video, presented by Kyle Kulinski, discusses reports and allegations concerning the construction of incinerators at detention centers established during the Trump administration. It focuses on the potential implications of these reports, drawing parallels to historical events and criticizing the administration's policies regarding immigration and detention. The video presents viewpoints and opinions rather than presenting definitive factual confirmations of the claims.

item.title: Thai Immigration Reviews Visa Compliance of Israeli Tourists in Pai
item.description: Thai immigration authorities in Mae Hong Son province have initiated investigations into the activities of Israeli residents and tourists in Pai following complaints that raised concerns about visa and work permit compliance. Pol Col Thanathorn Khamthiang, deputy commander of Immigration Division 3, stated that officials are coordinating with local police to ensure Israelis are not overstaying visas or working illegally. A preliminary review found no overstays, as most Israeli visitors use 60-day tourist visas, extendable by 30 days. Contrary to online claims, monthly Israeli tourist numbers are between 1,200 and 1,500, peaking at no more than 3,000 during cooler months. Authorities also inspected a gathering at the Chabad community center, attended by 200–300 Israelis, for potential violations, finding none. However, recent incidents, including vandalism by four Israelis at Pai Hospital, have caused tension. The individuals involved were fined, had visas revoked, deported, and banned

item.title: Estonian Intelligence Report Highlights Surge in Russia’s Ammunition Production and Imports
item.description:
According to the latest report published by Estonian intelligence services (VLA), Russia’s military-industrial complex operated at full capacity over the past year, producing more than seven million units of ammunition. This figure represents a seventeenfold increase compared to the beginning of the war in Ukraine in 2022. Estonian officials attribute part of this surge to the construction of new industrial facilities within Russian territory, enabling expanded domestic production despite the impact of international sanctions. Moscow is reported to have allocated more than 10 percent of its GDP to defense spending, reflecting a significant prioritization of military capabilities.
Since the start of the war, Russia’s artillery ammunition output has reportedly multiplied tenfold. In addition to boosting domestic production, Russia has relied on substantial ammunition imports. Since 2023, it has allegedly acquired between five and seven million shells from allied countries. Ukrainian ass

item.title: How Dashboard Cameras Provide Safety, Evidence, and Convenience for Families
item.description:
The article explores the growing reasons families are installing dashboard cameras in their vehicles for everyday use. It explains that modern driving often involves unpredictable situations, from sudden lane changes and confusing intersections to parking lot damage and shared vehicle use among family members. In such cases, dash cams offer an objective video record that can clarify events when memories differ.
Using the Vantrue N4 as an example of a three-channel camera system, the article outlines common features found in many dash cams, including loop recording, G-sensors that lock footage during impacts, and parking modes that continue monitoring while the vehicle is off. Families are encouraged to evaluate their specific needs—such as front, rear, or cabin coverage—while also considering privacy concerns and local regulations.
Video quality is emphasized as an important factor, but proper installation and maintenance are equally critical. Mounting position, windshield cleanlines

item.title: Songwriters Hall of Fame Inductee Billy Steinberg Dies at 74 After Cancer Battle
item.description:
Billy Steinberg, the acclaimed American songwriter whose lyrics helped define pop music in the 1980s and 1990s, has died at the age of 74. According to his attorney, Laurie Soriano, Steinberg passed away at his home in Los Angeles’ Brentwood neighborhood following a battle with cancer, as first reported by TMZ. Soriano remembered him not only as a gifted lyricist but also as a kind and genuine person whose work touched millions around the world.
Steinberg was best known for co-writing Madonna’s 1984 smash hit “Like a Virgin,” a song that became one of the most iconic pop anthems of its era. The track topped Billboard’s Hot 100 chart for six consecutive weeks between 1984 and 1985 and cemented Madonna’s global superstardom. Steinberg frequently collaborated with songwriter Tom Kelly, and together they produced a string of No. 1 hits. Among them were Cyndi Lauper’s “True Colors,” Heart’s “Alone,” Whitney Houston’s “So Emotional,” and The Bangles’ “Eternal Flame.”
His career began in th

item.title: Comprehensive Guide to the ALL Accor Loyalty Program
item.description: The ALL Accor loyalty program is often overlooked by U.S.-based travelers due to its limited presence in the United States and lack of a co-branded credit card. Despite this, it offers strong value for international travel, with over 5,700 properties worldwide including brands like Fairmont, Raffles, and Sofitel. Accor points are consistently valuable, redeemable at 2 euro cents per point for any room type, and can also cover on-property expenses such as meals, spa treatments, and incidentals. Points can be earned through hotel stays, car rentals, airline travel, and transfers from programs like Citi ThankYou, Capital One, Bilt, and Rove, though transfer ratios are below 1:1. Accor also features Limitless Experiences, allowing points to be redeemed for events and activities. Elite status is tiered from Silver to Diamond, earned through spending or nights stayed, with perks like lounge access, upgrades, and welcome drinks, though benefits are limited outside the Asia-Pacific region. Sub

item.title: Comprehensive Review of the Best Current Credit Card Offers
item.description: This article from Frequent Miler provides an in-depth overview of the top current credit card offers available as of February 2026. It covers both consumer and business credit cards, highlighting the first-year value of each offer and providing mini-reviews for practical usage. Featured cards include Alaska’s Atmos Rewards Summit Card with 100K points plus a 25K Companion Award, American Express Platinum Card offering up to 175K points and multiple credits, and Chase Sapphire Reserve® with 125K points. Other notable cards are American Express Gold Card, Southwest Rapid Rewards® consumer cards with Companion Passes, Capital One Venture Rewards Card, Delta SkyMiles® Amex cards, Marriott Bonvoy Boundless®, and Chase World of Hyatt Credit Card. Each card is analyzed for its perks, usability, annual fees, and potential value for frequent travelers or specific loyalty programs. The article emphasizes non-affiliate recommendations and considers long-term benefits, ongoing credits, and reward

item.title: How Visa Infinite Cardholders Can Claim Free Regal Movie Tickets and Popcorn
item.description: Visa Infinite cardholders can take advantage of a promotional offer through the Visa Infinite Concierge service to receive two free Regal movie tickets and popcorn. To qualify, cardholders must register an account with the concierge service using an eligible Visa Infinite card, including options like the Chase Sapphire Reserve®, Capital One Venture X, Chase Ritz-Carlton Visa Infinite, and US Bank Altitude Reserve. Once registered and verified via email, users can claim the offer and receive a voucher for redemption. The promotion is valid until December 31, 2026, but demand is expected to be high, so early registration is recommended. Certain restrictions apply: surcharges exist for IMAX®, RPX®, ScreenX®, 3D, and 4DX® films, as well as for theaters with luxury or VIP amenities. The vouchers cannot be used for private events, special screenings, or certain concerts and sporting events, and they are non-transferable, non-refundable, and only valid for one-time use. Vouchers can be redeem

item.title: Czech Designer Reimagines City Logos in Retro 1980s Style
item.description: Czech designer Kostya Petrenko has created a series of city logos inspired by the distinctive aesthetic of the 1980s. The project applies the era's characteristic slightly pixelated, analog glow to well-known cities such as Warsaw, Venice, Miami, and Sydney. Each logo captures the vibrant and energetic spirit of the 1980s while presenting modern cities in a nostalgic, retro format. Petrenko’s work highlights the interplay between historical design trends and contemporary graphic design, demonstrating how past styles can be reinterpreted in visually appealing ways today. The project has been showcased on Moss and Fog, with full collections available on Petrenko’s Behance page. The images showcase both artistic creativity and a deep understanding of retro aesthetics, making the series engaging for design enthusiasts and those interested in visual culture. Overall, this reinterpretation bridges the gap between historical visual styles and modern city branding, offering a playful yet authe

item.title: Spirit Airlines Introduces Free Spirit Debit Card with Monthly Fee and Limited Rewards
item.description:
Spirit Airlines is preparing to launch the Free Spirit Debit Card, with a landing page now live outlining most of its key features, although applications are not yet open. The card will carry a monthly fee of $6.99 and offer a modest welcome bonus of 200 Free Spirit points upon joining, plus 200 additional points per month during the first year of membership. Over 12 months, cardholders would earn between 2,400 and 2,600 bonus points while paying nearly $84 in fees.
In terms of earning structure, the debit card will provide 1 point per dollar spent in select categories and 1 point per $2 on all other purchases. Specific bonus categories have not yet been disclosed. Cardholders may also earn up to 4,000 bonus points per month based on their average account balance, though detailed requirements remain unclear.
Additional benefits include Group 2 priority boarding, priority check-in, and access to points pooling, a feature previously limited to elite members and Spirit credit card holde

item.title: US Bank Altitude Reserve May Be Product-Changed With Potential Annual Fee Refund
item.description: The US Bank Altitude Reserve card, once highly regarded for its rewards, experienced significant changes in December 2025 that reduced its overall appeal. Previously, the card offered 3X points on mobile wallet purchases, redeemable for 1.5 cents per point for travel, making it especially valuable for in-person spending, including warehouse store purchases. However, the card’s redemption options have been limited to 1 cent per point when deposited into a US Bank checking account, and its 3X rewards now have a monthly cap, reducing its overall attractiveness. Despite earlier promises of transferable points, US Bank has not provided updates on this program. A recent reader report indicates that product changing from the Altitude Reserve to the no-fee Altitude Connect card is possible through US Bank chat. In the example shared, the reader received a prorated refund of $33.33 from the $400 annual fee and a small statement credit for remaining points. The Altitude Connect card offers benef

item.title: Unitree Spring Festival Gala Robots —a Full Release of Additional Details
item.description: The video showcases a detailed look at the robots participating in Unitree's Spring Festival gala. It appears to be a demonstration and explanation of their performance, highlighting technical aspects and showcasing the robots' capabilities in a festive setting. The video provides additional information beyond initial announcements.

item.title: Capital One Shopping Offers High Cash Back Rates on Travel and Retail Purchases
item.description: Capital One Shopping, a public shopping portal that does not require a Capital One card, has recently rolled out a series of elevated cash back offers. Users have reported receiving targeted email promotions with 36% back at IHG Hotels & Resorts, 45% back at Sixt rental cars, 36% back at Hertz, 30% back at Lululemon, and even 90% back at Tuft & Needle. These offers often surpass typical cash back rates available through other portals, making them attractive for travel and shopping expenses. While rewards are only redeemable for gift cards and some deals may initially appear ineligible, many travel-related purchases have successfully transitioned to pending and credited status after completion. The platform’s home page occasionally features high-value deals, though email offers are generally more lucrative. Capital One Shopping is accessible to all users, regardless of credit card type, but tracking issues have been reported by some users. Nevertheless, many have accumulated significant

item.title: Comprehensive Review of the Chase Ink Business Cash Credit Card
item.description: The Chase Ink Business Cash Credit Card is a no-annual-fee business card that stands out for its high-value rewards and versatility. It offers 5X Chase Ultimate Rewards points on spending at cell phone, cable, office supply stores, and internet services, up to $25,000 annually. Additional earning includes 2X points on gas stations and restaurants and 1X points on all other purchases. Although marketed as a cash-back card, pairing it with a premium Chase Ultimate Rewards card, like the Ink Business Preferred or Sapphire Reserve, allows points to be transferred to hotel and airline partners, often exceeding 2 cents per point in value for hotel redemptions, particularly with Hyatt. The card includes a strong welcome offer of 75,000 points after $6,000 in spending within the first three months. Other notable perks include primary car rental insurance, purchase protection, and travel benefits. Its foreign transaction fee is 3%, which is a consideration for international use. The card is bes

item.title: Shopping Portals Offer Up to $300 or 25,000 Points for Opening a New Chime Account with Direct Deposit
item.description:
Shopping portals Rakuten and TopCashBack are currently featuring elevated rewards for customers who open a new Chime account and meet direct deposit requirements. Rakuten is offering either $250 cash back or 25,000 points, which can be taken as Amex Membership Rewards points or Bilt Rewards points, after completing two qualifying direct deposits. Alternatively, TopCashBack is offering $300 cash back for customers who open a Chime Checking Account and receive a qualifying direct deposit within 30 days of signing up.
To qualify through Rakuten, applicants must be new Chime members, set up payroll direct deposit, and receive their first paycheck within 30 days of enrollment. The initial payroll deposit must be at least $200 and must be made via ACH by an employer, payroll provider, or benefits payer. Chime operates as a financial technology company rather than a traditional bank, with banking services provided by partner institutions.
TopCashBack’s promotion similarly requires users to

item.title: Oscar-Winning Actor Robert Duvall Dies at 95 After Celebrated Six-Decade Career
item.description:
Robert Duvall, the acclaimed and versatile American actor known for his powerful, understated performances across film and television, has died at the age of 95. His wife, Luciana, announced that he passed away at their home on their Virginia ranch. Over a career spanning more than six decades, Duvall established himself as one of the finest actors of his generation, earning seven Academy Award nominations and winning the Oscar for Best Actor for his role as an alcoholic country singer in “Tender Mercies” (1983).
Duvall was widely recognized for his memorable performances in landmark films such as “The Godfather” (1972) and “The Godfather Part II” (1974), in which he portrayed the loyal family lawyer Tom Hagen, as well as for his iconic role as Lt. Col. Kilgore in “Apocalypse Now” (1979), delivering the legendary line about the “smell of napalm in the morning.” He also earned acclaim for his work in “The Great Santini,” “The Apostle,” “A Civil Action,” and the television miniseries “L

item.title: Guide to Choosing Additive-Free Electrolytes for Daily Hydration
item.description: Electrolytes are no longer just for athletes—they are increasingly used by people in daily routines, such as during long workdays, travel, or hot weather. As usage becomes frequent, many individuals are becoming more conscious of the ingredients in their hydration products. Additive-free electrolytes have gained popularity because they avoid sugars, artificial sweeteners, dyes, and other flavor enhancers, delivering essential minerals without altering the taste of water. Products like Buoy and Hy-Lyte provide unflavored drops that allow flexible dosing for daily hydration, fasting, or low-carb lifestyles. Trace Minerals also offers unsweetened drops focused on mineral content, while Transparent Labs Hydrate and Re Lyte cater to workout or intense activity needs with higher mineral content but may have some flavor. Pedialyte is useful for medical or short-term hydration but often contains sugar, making it less suitable for everyday use. Choosing the right additive-free electrolyte depen

item.title: The Role of the UK in Supporting the 1954 US-Backed Coup in Guatemala
item.description: Declassified documents reveal that in 1954, the United Kingdom played a key role in concealing the United States’ involvement in the overthrow of Guatemala’s democratically-elected government. Following a decade of democratic reforms after the 1944 ouster of a dictator, Guatemala faced opposition from regional autocrats and the American corporation United Fruit. When President Dwight Eisenhower authorized the CIA's Operation PBSUCCESS, US-backed forces pressured the Guatemalan military to depose President Jacobo Árbenz. The UK Foreign Office, fearing diplomatic repercussions and relying on US support for colonial interests, collaborated by suppressing international scrutiny, particularly at the United Nations. British officials produced a white paper framing the ousted government as Communist-dominated and minimizing US and corporate involvement. The UK also assisted the new regime led by Colonel Carlos Castillo Armas by lifting arms embargoes and sharing intelligence on exiled Guatema

item.title: JetBlue Flight + Cruise Packages Offer BOGO Airfare and Bonus Points
item.description: JetBlue is running a promotional offer for travelers booking Flight + Cruise packages through TrueBlue Vacations. The promotion includes a Buy One, Get One Free (BOGO) deal on base airfare for two passengers traveling together, valid for bookings made by April 30, 2026, and travel through December 31, 2026. Taxes and fees are not included in the BOGO discount, and Mint fares are excluded. In addition, bookings made between February 13–19, 2026, are eligible for 10,000 bonus TrueBlue points for either Flight + Cruise or cruise-only packages. These points are split among all passengers on the reservation, so each passenger receives a portion of the bonus. To qualify, each traveler must have a valid TrueBlue account. Furthermore, JetBlue Plus and JetBlue Business cardholders can receive a $100 statement credit on the purchase of a JetBlue Vacations package, allowing for potential stacking of multiple benefits. Travelers should carefully review cancellation and change policies, as penaltie

item.title: China Leads the World in Wind Power Production Despite Trump’s Claims
item.description:
During remarks at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, President Donald Trump criticized wind energy and claimed that China does not use the wind turbines it manufactures. Speaking to an audience of global leaders, Trump argued that although China produces and sells large numbers of wind turbines, it does not rely on them domestically for electricity generation. He suggested that China profits by selling turbines to other countries while avoiding their use at home.
However, data from the Global Wind Energy Council contradicts these assertions. By the end of 2024, China had installed 561 gigawatts of wind power capacity, making it the largest wind energy producer in the world by a wide margin. The United States ranks second in total installed capacity. Wind turbines generated an estimated 10 percent of China’s electricity in 2024, demonstrating that wind energy plays a significant role in the country’s power mix.
Trump’s comments are part of his broader criticism of renewab

item.title: United TravelBank Purchases May No Longer Qualify for Airline Incidental Fee Credits on Cards Like Amex Platinum
item.description: Recent reports, primarily from Doctor of Credit and user discussions on Reddit and Flyertalk, suggest that deposits to United Airlines' TravelBank no longer trigger airline incidental fee reimbursements on credit cards such as the American Express Platinum, which offers up to $200 annually for qualifying airline purchases. The change reportedly took effect around February 5 or 6, 2026, with some customer service representatives confirming a policy update and multiple data points showing no credits posting for recent purchases. TravelBank allows users to preload funds usable for United flights and services, with a 5-year validity, and has long served as a workaround to 'bank' these credits across years or cards by making purchases that code as incidental fees. The article cautions against immediate alarm, noting that similar reports of the method failing have occurred in past years—such as in 2021—only for reimbursements to resume after delays of weeks due to processing bottlenecks or t

item.title: LAYER and MAZZU Introduce Modular Spring-Based Camping Mattress Without Foam
item.description:
Design studio LAYER has partnered with MAZZU to rethink one of camping’s most familiar pieces of gear: the sleeping mattress. Rather than relying on traditional foam and air-filled structures, which often force users to compromise between comfort and portability, the collaboration proposes a structural redesign built around adaptability and repairability. The mattress replaces foam entirely with a grid of 72 small elastic spring units. Each spring operates independently, adjusting to the sleeper’s body and distributing weight more dynamically than standard foam pads.
This modular system aims to replicate the comfort of a home mattress while maintaining outdoor practicality. The components connect using simple snap-fit connectors and paracord, allowing the mattress to be assembled and disassembled with ease. When packed, the system collapses into a wheeled case that also functions as a campsite nightstand, adding a secondary utility to the design. A thin topper is included, not to add

item.title: Thousands of Immigrants Detained Illegally by ICE in First Five Months of Trump’s Term
item.description: Between October of the previous year and February 2026, U.S. immigration authorities (ICE) have been found to have illegally detained over 4,400 immigrants, according to federal court rulings. In many cases, immigrants remained in custody even after judges ordered their release. The total number of people under ICE custody has surged to 68,000, a 75% increase compared to the same period last year. The Trump administration's plan aimed to detain up to 3,000 individuals daily, targeting primarily immigrants without criminal records, contrary to official messaging equating immigration with crime. Since Trump’s current term began, over 20,200 habeas corpus petitions have been filed challenging indefinite detentions, with more than 4,400 cases ruled illegal by over 400 federal judges. In January alone, more than 6,000 habeas corpus petitions were filed, far exceeding previous monthly totals. Due to the sheer volume of legal challenges, ICE has often failed to comply with court orders, keepi

item.title: Aging Farmer Faces Health Challenges While Maintaining Multi-Generational Family Farm
item.description: Don Guinnip, a 74-year-old farmer in Marshall, Illinois, continues to work on the family farm that has been in his family since 1837, managing 1,000 acres of corn and soybeans along with 40 cattle. Despite decades of experience, his health issues—including prostate cancer and two hip replacements—make the physical demands of farming increasingly difficult. Unlike his children and siblings, who pursued careers outside agriculture, Don remains the sole family member dedicated to maintaining the farm. The Guinnip farm is held in a trust that splits ownership between Don and his four siblings, creating financial and succession challenges. With no clear successor interested in farming, options like dividing the land, forming an LLC, or leasing the farm are being considered. Don maintains low living costs and uses aging equipment to keep operations sustainable. His situation reflects a broader crisis in American agriculture: farmers are aging rapidly, commodity prices remain low, and farm ba

item.title: An Analysis of Michael Abrash’s Assembly Optimizations in Quake’s Software Renderer
item.description:
The article examines whether Michael Abrash’s hand-written x86 assembly code truly doubled the performance of Quake’s software renderer, as suggested by John Carmack’s original readme after the 1999 source code release. To verify the claim, the author compiled and benchmarked three versions of WinQuake on a Pentium MMX 233 MHz machine: the original assembly-optimized build, a rebuilt version with assembly enabled, and a version compiled entirely in C by disabling the id386 flag. Using a timedemo benchmark, the assembly-enabled builds achieved approximately 42 frames per second, while the pure C version ran at only 22.7 fps—almost exactly half the performance.
The investigation then breaks down the 63 assembly functions in the source tree, narrowing them to 32 relevant performance-critical routines related to drawing, rendering, math, and sound. By selectively enabling individual assembly routines, the author quantified their performance impact. The most significant gains came from low

item.title: An Analysis of Imperialism as a Global System of Economic, Military, and Political Control
item.description:
The article argues that imperialism constitutes a global system of domination rooted primarily in economic power, drawing heavily on Lenin’s theory in “Imperialism, the Highest Stage of Capitalism.” It explains that Lenin emphasized economic mechanisms—such as monopolies and financial capital—because they underpin political domination. According to the text, monopolies and financial capital enable powerful nations to dominate weaker ones, establishing a hierarchical global order divided between dominant and subordinated states. The author maintains that hegemony is enforced not only through military strength but also through economic instruments such as the US dollar, international financial institutions, and sanctions regimes.
The piece contends that the United States represents the leading imperialist power, citing its global military bases, influence over financial systems like SWIFT, and institutions such as the IMF and World Bank. It argues that capital flows are tightly controll

item.title: ‘Wuthering Heights’ achieves strong global box office debut despite mixed reviews
item.description: Emerald Fennell’s reinterpretation of Emily Brontë’s classic novel 'Wuthering Heights' has launched to impressive box office success, earning US$76.8 million in its opening weekend, making it the biggest debut of 2026 so far. The Warner Bros. romantic drama, starring Margot Robbie and Jacob Elordi, earned US$34.8 million domestically and US$42 million internationally, with numbers expected to rise further due to the President’s Day long weekend and upcoming releases in Japan, Vietnam, and China. Notably, the film has nearly recouped its $80 million production budget within its first weekend, though marketing costs are not included. Australia emerged as a key market, generating A$6.07 million (US$4.3 million), aided by the local popularity of the lead actors. Despite the financial success, the adaptation has polarized critics and audiences alike, holding a 63% rating on Rotten Tomatoes, a B CinemaScore, and a 51% definite recommendation from viewers. Audience demographics reveal a gende

item.title: EU Implements Rules to Prevent Destruction of Unsold Apparel and Footwear
item.description: The European Commission has introduced new measures under the Ecodesign for Sustainable Products Regulation (ESPR) aimed at preventing the destruction of unsold clothing, footwear, and accessories. Each year, an estimated 4-9% of textiles in Europe are destroyed without being worn, generating approximately 5.6 million tons of CO2 emissions—nearly equivalent to Sweden's total emissions in 2021. The new rules require companies to disclose information on discarded unsold goods and prohibit their destruction, with certain justified exceptions such as safety or product damage. Large companies must comply starting July 19, 2026, while medium-sized businesses will follow by 2030. To aid compliance, the European Commission has issued Delegated and Implementing Acts that clarify the allowed exceptions and provide a standardized format for reporting discarded stock, effective from February 2027. Companies are encouraged to explore alternative solutions such as resale, remanufacturing, donations,

item.title: Bianca Censori Named as Witness in Kanye West’s Legal Case Over Former Employee Claims
item.description: Bianca Censori has been called as a potential witness in her husband Kanye West’s ongoing legal dispute with his former project manager, Tony Saxon. Saxon’s lawsuit alleges unsafe and unethical working conditions during his employment at West’s Malibu property, including excessively long 16-hour workdays and responsibilities beyond a standard project manager role. He also claims he was tasked with hazardous assignments, such as removing windows and electricity from the property and installing large indoor generators, which he considered extremely dangerous. Saxon stated that West warned him that failure to comply would result in termination. West has denied all allegations, asserting in court filings that any harm Saxon experienced was not his responsibility and that the claims do not constitute wrongdoing. The role Censori will play as a witness is currently unclear, but her involvement may add a new dimension to the case as the trial approaches, scheduled for February 21. The legal p

item.title: Maya Hawke and Christian Lee Hutson Wed in Intimate Valentine’s Day Ceremony in NYC
item.description: Actor and musician Maya Hawke and singer-songwriter Christian Lee Hutson have officially married, celebrating their union on Valentine’s Day at St. George’s Episcopal Church in New York City. The wedding was both intimate and high-profile, attended by family, friends, and notable figures from the entertainment industry. Hawke’s parents, actors Uma Thurman and Ethan Hawke, were present, with Ethan Hawke walking his daughter down the aisle. Following the church ceremony, the couple hosted a reception at The Players’ Members Club in Manhattan, where guests enjoyed the celebration for hours. The event also featured a mini reunion of the cast of Netflix’s Stranger Things, including Finn Wolfhard, Noah Schnapp, Joe Keery, Caleb McLaughlin, Gaten Matarazzo, Sadie Sink, and Natalia Dyer. Hawke, known for her role as Robin Buckley on Stranger Things, has developed a career in both acting and music, while Hutson is a respected singer-songwriter. The couple, publicly linked since 2023, have colla

item.title: Israeli Parliament Considers Mandatory Death Penalty for Palestinians Accused of Killing Israelis
item.description: In November of the previous year, the Israeli parliament approved in its first reading a bill that would make the death penalty mandatory for Palestinians convicted of killing Israeli citizens. This legislation effectively removes judicial discretion, preventing military judges from imposing alternative sentences such as life imprisonment. In anticipation of potential approval, Israeli prison authorities have reportedly begun preparing a dedicated facility to carry out executions. According to reports from i24News, the prison administration is planning a specialized, isolated complex for this purpose. Critics argue that the law is discriminatory, applying exclusively to Palestinians and not to Israeli citizens, particularly settlers who kill Palestinians. The bill could extend to Palestinians involved in attacks in the West Bank, while judicial safeguards for Palestinians under military courts are minimal, raising concerns about potential mass executions. Observers also note that the l

item.title: Former Trump Supporter Expresses Disillusionment Over Political Corruption and Child Abuse Allegations
item.description: A former supporter of President Donald Trump, Blake Humphreys, has gone viral on Facebook after posting a detailed and emotional message criticizing the president for failing to address corruption and protect children. Humphreys explained that he had been one of Trump’s most loyal supporters, defending him through investigations, indictments, and controversies because he believed Trump would 'drain the swamp' and confront elite corruption. However, Humphreys’ support ended after Trump allegedly dismissed the importance of the Jeffrey Epstein case, suggesting it was 'time to move on'. Humphreys questioned how anyone could ignore serious allegations of child trafficking and abuse, accusing political leaders of consistently protecting the powerful while covering up the truth. He criticized both major parties, claiming that cultural and political divisions are deliberately manufactured to benefit elites, and stated he no longer identifies with either party, declaring he will never vote aga

item.title: Democratizing the Economy to Address Climate and Social Crises
item.description: The article argues that the current capitalist system is inherently incapable of addressing the pressing social and ecological crises of the 21st century. Capitalism prioritizes profit maximization over human needs or ecological sustainability, resulting in overproduction of luxury goods and underproduction of essential services such as affordable housing, public transit, and renewable energy infrastructure. Fossil fuels remain subsidized and more profitable than cheaper renewable alternatives, perpetuating environmental harm. The system also relies on global inequality, exploiting cheap labor and resources from the global south through mechanisms like debt, sanctions, and military interventions. The authors propose that transforming the economy into a democratically governed system can help prioritize social and ecological goals. They outline three necessary steps: first, establishing a new financial architecture with public investment banks that direct funds toward sustainable projec

item.title: Comprehensive 2026 Overview of IHG One Rewards: Elite Status, Benefits, and Redemption Strategies
item.description:
The article provides a comprehensive 2026 guide to IHG One Rewards, the loyalty program of InterContinental Hotels Group, covering its structure, benefits, elite status tiers, credit card perks, and redemption strategies. IHG One Rewards allows members to earn points on paid stays booked directly through IHG and redeem them for free night awards. The program no longer uses a fixed award chart, meaning redemption rates are dynamic and can range widely, sometimes exceeding 200,000 points per night. Members can also take advantage of periodic discounts, including 15% off select award stays and discounts at newly opened properties.
A key feature is the 4th Night Free benefit for co-branded credit card holders, which can generate substantial savings, especially during high-demand periods. IHG also issues various free night certificates, including 40,000-point anniversary certificates that can be topped up with additional points.
Elite status ranges from Silver to Diamond, with additional

item.title: Photographer Captures Meteor Appearing to Enter Mount Merapi During Long-Exposure Shot
item.description: Photographer Gunarto Song captured a striking long-exposure photograph of Mount Merapi, a highly active stratovolcano in Indonesia, showing a meteor streaking across the night sky. While the meteor most likely passed behind the volcano, the perspective of Song’s photo makes it appear as if the meteor is flying directly into the volcano's crater. The image, taken with a four-second exposure, highlights the glowing trail of the meteor and the volcanic landscape in a dramatic composition reminiscent of science fiction imagery. Mount Merapi has been erupting intermittently since 1548 and remains one of Indonesia's most active volcanoes. This photograph demonstrates how timing, perspective, and photographic technique can combine to create visually stunning natural phenomena. The article draws a comparison to a similar meteor photograph captured on the Kamchatka Peninsula in 2016, showing that such rare moments can occasionally be recorded with careful planning or luck. The photograph is cop

item.title: Essential Safeguards for a Successful Freelance Career
item.description: Freelancing offers independence and flexibility but comes with unique challenges that require careful planning. Transitioning from traditional employment to independent work removes the safety net of an employer, requiring freelancers to assess their risks, including client disputes, missed payments, equipment issues, or legal challenges. Establishing clear contracts is critical to protect professional interests, set expectations, define deliverables, and provide mechanisms for dispute resolution. Financial stability is another key component; freelancers should maintain separate business accounts, track records, plan for taxes, budget for slow periods, and consider retirement savings. Insurance, such as professional liability and business property coverage, serves as a vital safeguard against unexpected events and potential claims, offering peace of mind and confidence. Beyond formal protections, building a strong support network of mentors, peers, legal or financial advisors, and free

item.title: Strategic Responses of Russia and China to U.S. Operations in Latin America
item.description: In spring 2026, the U.S. launched a major military operation in Venezuela, including the capture of President Nicolás Maduro, under the stated aim of defending democracy and countering the influence of rival powers. The article explores why Russia and China have not responded with equivalent military action. From Moscow and Beijing's perspective, direct military confrontation in America's traditional sphere of influence carries prohibitive costs and limited benefits. Instead, they pursue a strategic approach of indirect pressure, focusing on areas where the U.S. is more vulnerable, such as Ukraine for Russia and the Indo-Pacific for China. Both countries also coordinate to avoid uncontrolled escalation. The strategy includes building alternatives to the U.S.-dominated global economic system, deepening bilateral cooperation in energy and finance, and developing long-term economic resilience against sanctions. Russia leverages agreements in Africa for resource and weapons exchange, while

item.title: Nicole Kidman Shares Peaceful Galentine’s Day Post Following Divorce
item.description:
Nicole Kidman marked Galentine’s Day with a calm and intimate social media post, embracing a message of self-love and quiet reflection during her first Valentine’s season since her reported divorce from Keith Urban. The actress shared a photo of herself sitting on her bed in a relaxed setting, wearing a long-sleeved striped shirt with a blanket draped over her legs. Bathed in sunlight with her eyes closed, Kidman appeared serene and content, projecting a sense of peace and renewal.
Rather than opting for a glamorous or extravagant display, the post focused on simplicity and personal comfort. Her caption, wishing followers a “Happy Galentines,” highlighted the celebration of female friendships and love beyond romantic relationships. The understated tone of the image, combined with her signature polished look—featuring bold red lipstick and soft glam makeup—created a balance between casual intimacy and classic Hollywood elegance.
The article frames this moment as symbolic of a fresh ch

item.title: Colombian Congresswoman Ángela Vergara Appeals for Help After Son Detained by ICE
item.description: Colombian Congresswoman Ángela Vergara has publicly appealed for help after her son, Rafael Alfonso Vergara, was detained by U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). In a video shared on social media, Vergara appeared emotional and stated that her son has been held for 18 days in what she described as harsh and degrading conditions. She emphasized that her son has no criminal record, possesses a legal work permit, a social security number, and is awaiting an asylum hearing in 2028. Vergara highlighted the contrast between her previous public support for ICE raids and her current personal ordeal, noting the human impact of prolonged detentions on law-abiding individuals. Her political party, the Colombian Conservative Party, expressed solidarity with her and underscored that many Colombians face similar challenges abroad while navigating legal processes. The story has sparked online debate, especially regarding Vergara's prior support for U.S. President Donald Trump and ICE opera

item.title: Wall Street Journal Report Details Alleged Mismanagement and Conflicts of Interest at DHS Under Kristi Noem
item.description: A recent Wall Street Journal report has raised serious concerns about alleged corruption and mismanagement within the US Department of Homeland Security (DHS) under Secretary Kristi Noem. The report highlights Noem’s close relationship with top adviser Corey Lewandowski, who is reportedly romantically involved with her despite both being married. Lewandowski, classified as a special government employee with a service cap of 130 days per year, has been accused of taking excessive control over DHS contracting processes, creating opacity and raising alarms within the department and the White House. Both Noem and Lewandowski have allegedly used a luxury 737 MAX jet for travel, with DHS currently leasing the aircraft and considering purchasing it for $70 million, double the cost of other planes being acquired for deportation efforts. The report also claims the pair have exhibited abusive behavior toward DHS staff, including frequent berating, polygraph tests for mistrusted employees, and fi

item.title: British Museum reviews and updates Middle East gallery terminology after concerns raised by UK Lawyers for Israel
item.description:
The British Museum has confirmed that it is reviewing and updating terminology used in some of its Middle East gallery displays after concerns were raised by UK Lawyers for Israel (UKLFI), a UK-based advocacy group. According to statements cited in the report, the group argued that certain historical references to “Palestine” in exhibitions covering the ancient Levant and Egypt were inaccurate or potentially misleading when applied across long historical periods. UKLFI maintained that using a single modern geographical term to describe regions over thousands of years risked oversimplifying complex historical changes and creating an impression of continuity that did not reflect scholarly understanding.
In response, a spokesperson for the British Museum stated that audience testing had shown that the historic use of the term “Palestine” might not be meaningful in some contexts. The museum is therefore reviewing panels and labels on a case-by-case basis. For example, displays in the Leva

item.title: Two Exceptionally Rare Roman Mithraic Altars from Scotland to be Exhibited
item.description: Two exceptionally rare Roman Mithraic altars discovered in 2010 at Inveresk, East Lothian, Scotland, are being displayed publicly for the first time at the National Museum of Scotland in Edinburgh. These altars are unique, not only as the only Roman altars found in Scotland, but also as some of the finest examples of Roman sculpture in Roman Britain. Dating to the 140s A.D. during Emperor Antoninus Pius’ occupation, they predate most other Mithraic artifacts in Britain by over a century. The altars were central to a northernmost Mithraeum in the Roman Empire, likely a dark, cave-like temple. One altar is dedicated to Mithras, featuring carvings of a raven, griffin, lyre, and other Mithraic symbols, while the second altar honors Sol Invictus, designed with cutouts allowing light to illuminate the sun god's features in the temple’s darkness. Both altars show traces of their original polychrome paint. The exhibition, 'Roman Scotland: Life on the Edge of Empire,' will run from November 14,

item.title: Federal Judge Dismisses Minneapolis ICE Assault Case After Video Contradicts Officers’ Testimony
item.description:
A federal judge has dismissed with prejudice the charges against two Venezuelan men accused of assaulting Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officers during a January 14 incident in Minneapolis, after newly surfaced video evidence contradicted sworn testimony from two agents. The men, Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis and Alfredo Alejandro Aljorna, had been charged with forcibly assaulting, resisting, or impeding federal officers following a confrontation that ended with Sosa-Celis suffering a gunshot wound to the leg.
Initially, the Department of Homeland Security described a chaotic scene involving a car chase, a physical struggle, and multiple attackers wielding objects such as a snow shovel and broom handle. An officer reportedly fired in self-defense, claiming he feared for his life. However, as court documents evolved, aspects of the narrative shifted, including the number of alleged assailants and who had fled in the vehicle.
ICE Acting Director Todd Lyons later acknowledged that

item.title: Ukrainian Artist Creates Elegant Animal Sculptures from Wood and Resin
item.description: Ukrainian artist Yurii Myketka, working under the label WoodWhaleLab, crafts striking animal sculptures using a combination of wood and resin. His creations include birds, whales, and dogs, each designed with a minimalist yet expressive style that captures the essence of the animal without unnecessary detail. The artworks feature a dual-tone aesthetic, where the natural textures of the wood are paired with colorful resin to create visually compelling pieces. Myketka's skill in sculpture is evident in the smooth, streamlined forms and the careful balance between simplicity and character. These sculptures are not only visually appealing but also demonstrate the artist's understanding of form, anatomy, and material interplay. For those interested in collecting or purchasing, a wide selection of his works is available on Etsy, allowing art enthusiasts worldwide to bring a piece of Myketka's craftsmanship into their homes. His work has gained attention for its modern, minimalist aesthetic,

item.title: NASA and SpaceX Launch Crew-12 to the ISS Following Early Return of Crew-11
item.description:
On February 13, 2026, NASA’s Crew-12 mission successfully launched aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 Block 5 rocket from Space Launch Complex 40 (SLC-40) at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida. The mission restored a U.S. crew presence aboard the International Space Station (ISS) after the early return of Crew-11 due to a medical issue left only three Soyuz MS-28 cosmonauts on board. The Crew Dragon capsule Freedom (C212), flying for the fifth time, carried four astronauts: NASA commander Jessica Meir, NASA pilot Jack Hathaway, ESA mission specialist Sophie Adenot of France, and Roscosmos cosmonaut Andrey Fedyaev.
The first stage booster, B1101, completed its second flight and landed at the newly inaugurated Landing Zone 40 (LZ-40), expanding SpaceX’s recovery infrastructure at Cape Canaveral. Following a nominal ascent and a second-stage burn lasting over six minutes, the spacecraft entered its target orbit and docked with the Harmony module’s zenith port (PMA-3/IDA-3) on February 1

item.title: Electric Bicycle Co. Files for Chapter 7 Bankruptcy, Citing Tariffs and Financial Pressures
item.description:
Electric Bicycle Co. (EBC), a California-based assembler of custom electric bicycles, has filed for Chapter 7 bankruptcy liquidation, reporting $6.1 million in liabilities and $953,000 in assets. Founder Sean Lupton-Smith attributed the company’s financial collapse largely to rising U.S. tariffs on imported components. According to Lupton-Smith, tariffs on key parts increased from 25% to as high as 55%, significantly raising production costs. He also pointed to financing charges and competitive pressures from companies allegedly bypassing tariffs through third countries or under-invoicing products.
Founded about 14 years ago, EBC focused on producing customizable e-bikes with as much U.S.-made content as possible in an effort to reduce exposure to import duties. At its Newport Beach facility, employees painted frames and components to order, assembled battery packs, built wheels, and completed final assembly. Despite these efforts, Lupton-Smith acknowledged that a portion of component

item.title: Hideki Sato, Key Engineer Behind Sega Consoles, Dies at 77
item.description: Hideki Sato, widely recognized as the 'Father of Sega Hardware,' passed away on February 13, 2026, at the age of 77. Joining Sega in 1971, Sato witnessed the company’s transformation from a U.S.-based electronics firm into a global gaming powerhouse. He was instrumental in the development of Sega's iconic consoles, including the SG-1000, Master System, Mega Drive (Genesis in North America), Sega Saturn, and Dreamcast. Promoted to director of Sega's Research and Development department in 1989, Sato led the team tasked with challenging Nintendo during the 16-bit era. He emphasized blending arcade quality with home console affordability and designed the Mega Drive with a distinctive, high-quality aesthetic that helped establish Sega as a household name. Beyond the console wars, Sato’s influence shaped the evolution of gaming hardware, leaving an enduring legacy. His work continues to inspire developers and gamers worldwide, and his contributions are celebrated in interviews, articles, and

item.title: Measles outbreak infects over 60 children in Enfield amid low vaccination rates
item.description: A measles outbreak has affected more than 60 children in north London, particularly in the Enfield area, where cases have been reported across seven schools and a nursery. Several children required hospital treatment, raising concerns over the potential for a wider outbreak across London due to low MMR (measles, mumps, rubella) vaccination coverage. Enfield's director of public health, Dudu Sher-Arami, warned that the city has some of the lowest vaccination uptake rates in the UK, which could allow the virus to spread rapidly. Measles is highly contagious, capable of infecting up to 18 unvaccinated individuals per case, and can lead to serious complications such as deafness, brain damage, or hospitalization in one in five children. Temporary vaccination clinics have been set up in schools across Enfield to increase inoculation rates, and a government campaign promoting childhood vaccination is planned with social media, radio, and online advertising. The outbreak highlights a broader p

item.title: Report Claims Over 50,000 Foreign Nationals Served in Israeli Forces During Gaza War
item.description:
The article reports that more than 50,000 individuals holding foreign passports served in the Israeli army during the war in Gaza. Citing data published by the investigative outlet Declassified UK, it argues that foreign participation was not limited to isolated volunteers but formed part of a broader transnational network. According to the figures presented, the largest contingent allegedly came from the United States, followed by France, Russia, Germany, and Ukraine. Additional participants were said to come from the United Kingdom, Romania, Canada, Italy, the Netherlands, Brazil, Argentina, Australia, South Africa, Ethiopia, Switzerland, Belgium, Austria, Spain, Hungary, and the Czech Republic.
The article highlights potential legal implications in several countries, noting that some national laws prohibit or restrict citizens from serving in foreign armed forces, particularly when those forces are engaged in conflicts involving states at peace with their home country. In the Unite

item.title: Eradicating Invasive Yellow Crazy Ants on Johnston Atoll
item.description: Johnston Atoll, a remote group of islands in the Pacific Ocean under U.S. jurisdiction, has a turbulent history marked by military use, nuclear testing, and chemical weapons storage. After decades of abandonment, the islands became a sanctuary for seabirds. However, in 2010, invasive yellow crazy ants (Anoplolepis gracilipes) appeared, threatening the fragile ecosystem by preying on bird eggs and disrupting native species. Known for their acid-spitting defense and highly collaborative colonies, these ants posed a severe ecological risk. The U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service launched an extensive eradication program, employing volunteer teams called the Crazy Ant Strike Team (CAST). Over a decade, through innovative methods including insecticide-laced cat food and sugar hydrogel crystals, the teams successfully eradicated the ants by 2021, restoring the atoll's ecological balance. Proposals to use Johnston Atoll for SpaceX rocket testing were ultimately rejected to preserve the island's wild

item.title: US Department of Justice Confirms Compilation of Domestic Terrorism List
item.description: On February 15, 2026, U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi acknowledged, for the first time, the existence of a secret list of 'domestic terrorist organizations' during a House of Representatives hearing. When asked, Bondi confirmed that Antifa is included on the list, which is compiled under the Trump administration's National Security Presidential Memorandum 7 (NSPM-7). The list identifies certain political movements and individuals as 'domestic terrorists,' a term not formally defined in U.S. law, encompassing groups opposing traditional American values or government policies. Bondi has instructed the FBI to maintain a record of groups potentially involved in domestic terrorism, with reports initially due January 3 and updated every 30 days. Congressional inquiries about the list were met with evasion, and the FBI declined to comment on its contents or the people included. The article highlights that the list could encompass Americans who have not committed violent acts but hold dissenti

item.title: Rising Tensions Between Niger and France Amid Military and Economic Disputes
item.description:
A senior Nigerien military official, General Amadou Ibro, stated during a large public rally in Niamey that Niger is preparing for a potential conflict with France. Speaking before a crowd in a stadium, Ibro declared that although the country had not previously been at war, it was now moving toward confrontation with France. The audience responded with applause and anti-French chants, and footage of the speech quickly spread on social media.
French military officials dismissed the remarks as part of an 'information war.' Colonel Guillaume Vernet, spokesperson for the French armed forces, emphasized that French troops had already withdrawn from Niger at the end of 2023, concluding their involvement in Operation Barkhane, which targeted jihadist groups in the Sahel region.
Relations between Niger and France have deteriorated significantly since the July 2023 military coup that ousted President Mohamed Bazoum. Following the coup, Niger expelled French forces and suspended military coope

item.title: Contrasting State Responses to Protests in Spain
item.description: The article analyzes two contrasting protest events in Spain to highlight perceived inconsistencies in the state's response. The first case involves a large group of farmers blocking the A-52 highway near Xinzo de Limia for weeks using heavy machinery, causing significant disruption to traffic and damage to infrastructure. Despite the severity of the action, the state response was minimal, with no police intervention and apparent impunity. In contrast, four years ago, climate activists from Futuro Vegetal blocked a single lane of the M-30 highway in Madrid to protest meat industry subsidies and environmental concerns. This protest, although nonviolent and motivated by altruistic reasons, led to immediate police intervention, arrests, and charges including participation in a criminal organization and public disorder. The author argues that the difference in state response—leniency for farmers versus harsh treatment of climate activists—reflects systemic bias and disproportionality in Sp

item.title: Highlights from LGA’s Capital One Lounge, Airline Rankings, Qatar Airways Amenities, and Must-Try Foods
item.description: This week’s roundup from Frequent Miler covers several travel and food-related highlights. Dave Grossman from MilesTalk provides a first look at Capital One’s new Landing Lounge at LGA Terminal B, describing it as a spacious 12,500 square-foot area resembling a high-quality tapas restaurant with a menu created alongside celebrity chef José Andrés. Venture X cardholders enjoy unlimited complimentary access, while guest visits cost $45. The lounge prioritizes casual dining over extended lounging, with food as the main attraction. Travel + Leisure recently ranked airlines for long-haul flights based on reliability, comfort, entertainment, and dining, placing Singapore Airlines at number one, Delta at ninth, and both United and American Airlines at the bottom. Qatar Airways business class passengers can take home a small supplementary pillow with inspirational quotes, a rare exception to the usual policy of leaving bedding on board. Finally, the article highlights unique regional foods, su

item.title: Senate Democrats Introduce Bill to Prohibit Surveillance and Surge Pricing in Large Grocery Stores
item.description:
Two Democratic senators, Sen. Ben Ray Luján of New Mexico and Sen. Jeff Merkley of Oregon, have introduced the Stop Price Gouging in Grocery Stores Act of 2026, a bill aimed at banning surveillance and surge pricing practices in large grocery stores. The proposed legislation would require retailers to disclose any use of facial recognition technology and would prohibit the use of electronic shelf labels (ESLs) in major grocery outlets. Lawmakers argue that ESLs make it easier for stores to change prices remotely and potentially connect them to algorithms capable of adjusting prices dynamically based on factors such as time of day, demand, or even the identity of the shopper.
Critics of these technologies warn that they could enable personalized pricing strategies that discriminate against customers based on race, gender, income level, or location. Concerns intensified after a 2025 study found that Instacart charged customers varying prices for identical products, in some cases up to 2

item.title: Chase Offers 40% Transfer Bonus to Virgin Atlantic Flying Club
item.description: Chase is offering a 40% bonus on transfers of Ultimate Rewards points to Virgin Atlantic Flying Club, valid until February 28, 2026. This promotion mirrors a similar bonus from November, which then shifted to a 30% bonus. This limited-time offer could appeal to those with a large amount of Chase Ultimate Rewards points, especially if they're not often used for Hyatt stays or other Chase-specific rewards. However, users should be cautious when transferring to Virgin Atlantic as the airline is known for devaluations that may impact the value of points. Virgin Points can be redeemed for valuable awards, such as ANA First Class flights to Japan for as low as 72.5K points, or flights to London in economy from 6K points. However, some fees can be high, especially on UK-originating flights. It's important to check award availability before transferring points, as transfers are typically instantaneous. Be sure to consider Virgin's potential devaluations before committing.

item.title: Maximize Savings During the Volaris Anniversary Sale
item.description: The Volaris anniversary sale, celebrated every March, is a highly anticipated event for travelers looking to save on flights. With discounted fares on both domestic and international routes, travelers can explore destinations in Mexico and beyond at more affordable prices. The sale typically lasts for weeks, offering ample opportunity for passengers to find the best deals. These promotions include perks for loyalty members, such as extra points or member-only pricing. To make the most of the sale, travelers should be flexible with their dates, book in advance, and use flight comparison tools to track fare changes. Popular destinations like Tulum, Monterrey, and Puerto Escondido are often featured, and there are additional ways to save throughout the year. By staying updated on promotions and booking early, travelers can enjoy their dream destinations without stretching their budget.

item.title: A Guide to Paying Taxes Using Your Credit Card
item.description: Paying taxes by credit card is a strategy that many people use to increase their spending on rewards cards. This video explains the process, the potential fees involved, and the pros and cons of using a credit card for tax payments. It covers how to make tax payments, whether it's for estimated taxes or end-of-year taxes, and what fees are typically charged by payment processors. Viewers will learn about two main processors for these transactions and the importance of understanding the percentage fee they might incur. The video also highlights a PayPal trick for certain payments and offers tips for maximizing rewards. While it can be a smart move for those looking to earn points, it's crucial to weigh the cost of the transaction fees against the value of the rewards earned. The video provides useful insights for anyone interested in paying taxes via credit card, including an overview of the tools and strategies that can be used effectively.

item.title: Exploring Vintage Valentine's Day Cards from the 1940s
item.description: Valentine’s Day has always been a time to express affection, and the tradition of exchanging cards has been a charming part of this celebration for many. In this article, we take a nostalgic journey through a collection of vintage Valentine’s Day cards, most of which hail from the 1940s. These cards are characterized by their use of playful puns and endearing messages, with each one showcasing a delightful and sometimes corny sense of humor. While some of these cards might be considered a bit old-fashioned today, they still carry a warmth and sweetness that is both timeless and nostalgic. The cards were designed to bring smiles, with exaggerated expressions of affection and witty wordplay that were popular in the mid-20th century. As the author notes, 'the cornier the pun, the sweeter the sentiment.' The collection not only gives us a glimpse into the past but also highlights the charmingly simple yet heartfelt way people used to send their love. If you're a fan of vintage design and a

item.title: Cardi B Falls Off Stage Chair and Jokes About Government After Incident
item.description: Cardi B had an unexpected fall during the opening night of her 'Little Miss Drama Tour.' While attempting a dramatic move, she slipped off her chair but immediately recovered with humor, claiming 'That wasn’t me, that was the government.' This lighthearted comment followed recent criticisms of the government by the rapper regarding immigration policies. Cardi B also engaged directly with her audience, discussing issues related to ICE and voicing her support for immigrant rights. Her remarks, which included a bold statement warning about ICE officials, sparked both attention and controversy. Some Homeland Security officials reportedly took issue with her comments, recalling a past controversy involving Cardi's criminal history. Despite the mix of humor and political remarks, the fall and her response turned her concert into a memorable event. Cardi’s unpredictable nature, both on stage and in her statements, ensured her tour opener would be anything but ordinary.

item.title: UK Court Declares Palestine Action Ban Illegal, Thousands Affected
item.description: A London High Court ruling on February 13, 2026, declared the UK's ban on the group Palestine Action to be illegal, marking a significant setback for the Labour government. The ban, enforced under anti-terrorism laws, was deemed disproportionate and unlawful. The group, which has protested against Israeli arms manufacturer Elbit Systems, was accused of causing financial damage to the company. Since the proscription, over 2,500 people have been detained for supporting the group, and while police will stop arrests for displaying support, the legal limbo remains for those charged under the terrorism act. Huda Ammori, co-founder of Palestine Action, hailed the ruling as a victory for freedom of expression and direct action. The government intends to appeal, with critics highlighting that the proscription was politically motivated, intended to appease pro-Israel groups. The ruling is a significant moment in the debate over civil liberties and protest rights in the UK, especially regarding t

item.title: Overview of The Jmail Encyclopedia and Epstein Email Archive Profiles
item.description: The Jmail Encyclopedia, known as Jikipedia, is a digital repository documenting individuals, places, and events related to the Epstein scandal. Based on AI-generated profiles, it synthesizes information directly from government-released emails and documents. The encyclopedia aims to provide researchers, journalists, and the public with detailed insights into the network surrounding Epstein. It currently contains 63 articles, including featured profiles such as Lesley Groff, who appears in over 230,000 emails, reflecting their centrality in the email archive. Other high-volume email correspondents include Rich Kahn, Karyna Shuliak, Larry Visoski, Bella Klein, and Ann Rodriguez. Beyond individual profiles, the encyclopedia covers key locations such as Zorro Ranch in New Mexico, Epstein's properties in Palm Beach and Manhattan, and Paris apartments. It also documents relevant legal and investigative topics, including criminal statutes, flight logs, banking relationships, prosecution roadm

item.title: Discord Trials Age Verification with Persona, Raising Privacy Concerns
item.description: Discord has announced plans for a global age verification rollout, which will require users to submit either facial scans or government-issued IDs to confirm their age. The rollout, set for March, has already sparked backlash from users concerned about privacy and data security. Some UK users are now part of a test using Persona, an age verification vendor whose lead investor is Founders Fund, co-founded by Peter Thiel. Thiel is also known for co-founding Palantir, a surveillance technology firm, and his controversial history in the tech and finance world. Discord states that data submitted to Persona will only be temporarily stored for up to seven days before deletion. While the company insists this measure is limited to certain users and is intended to improve age detection, critics remain wary due to Thiel's involvement and the sensitive nature of the information being collected. The Electronic Frontier Foundation and other privacy advocates have expressed concern over potential mis

item.title: Bill Maher Criticizes Republican Backlash to Bad Bunny’s Super Bowl Halftime Performance
item.description:
During the February 13 episode of 'Real Time With Bill Maher,' host Bill Maher addressed the backlash from some conservatives over Bad Bunny’s Super Bowl Halftime Show performance. Maher argued that much of the outrage from Republican commentators and supporters was exaggerated and inconsistent. He joked that conservative critics were still “butt-hurt” over the show and suggested their complaints had shifted over time. According to Maher, some initially criticized the performance for being in Spanish, while others later claimed their objections were about its sexual content and explicit lyrics.
Maher mocked former President Donald Trump’s remarks about not understanding the lyrics, comparing the experience to listening to Mick Jagger, whose words he said he also often could not understand but still enjoyed. Although Maher admitted he did not understand the Spanish-language performance either, he said that did not prevent him from appreciating the show’s energy and spectacle. He likene

item.title: Travel Disruptions, Bilt 2.0 Issues, and Loyalty Program Insights: Frequent Miler’s Weekly Review
item.description:
In this weekly roundup, Frequent Miler covers a range of developments in the travel and points-and-miles world, mixing personal experience with industry updates. Nick Reyes opens with a cautionary tale about booking a nonrefundable cash flight just days before departure. When his son fell ill shortly afterward, the family had to cancel. Fortunately, trip cancellation protections from a credit card are expected to cover the loss, underscoring the importance of built-in travel insurance benefits when flexibility is limited.
